about.credits.body:
InkOtter is developed as a focused open printing tool for closed label printers.

Development: Arne von Irmer and ChatGPT 5.4.
The project lives in the public repository at github.com/dasarne/inkotter.
GUI, rendering, protocol materialization, and transport are powered by the Python implementation in this repository.
Contributions for additional printers are explicitly welcome.
about.libraries.body:
InkOtter uses these key libraries:

- PySide6 for the desktop GUI
- Pillow for image handling
- CairoSVG and optionally Inkscape for SVG rasterization
- Python stdlib modules for configuration, metadata, and platform inspection
about.license.body:
InkOtter is released under the MIT License.

The full license text is available in the top-level LICENSE file of the repository.
about.privacy.body:
InkOtter is a local desktop application and does not track its users.

It does not require cloud services for rendering, previewing, or printing. InkOtter does not upload documents to third-party servers, does not build user profiles, and does not embed analytics or telemetry backends.

Recent document history is stored only locally in ~/.config/inkotter/gui_recent_documents.json. Bluetooth discovery and printing communicate directly with the selected device.

Privacy is also part of the motivation: according to MITM-proxy analysis, the related vendor app showed location-related permission and network behavior around Bluetooth workflows. InkOtter deliberately avoids that app dependency and stays a local desktop tool.
